book: 國中英語超強字彙 Super 2500

senses.en: I've left my whiteout at home; can I borrow yours?
senses.zh_tr: 我把立可白留在家裡；可否把你的借給我？

senses.en: What did you buy for your girlfriend on Valentine's Day?
senses.zh_tr: 你在情人節買了什麼給你的女朋友？

senses.en: There are a hundred cents in a dollar.
senses.zh_tr: 一元裡有一百分錢。

senses.en: Here is your change.
senses.zh_tr: 這裡是你的零錢。

senses.en: The fur coat is too cheap to be true.
senses.zh_tr: 這件皮外套太便宜了，可能不是真的。

senses.en: The tickets to that circus cost too much, and they sold very poorly.
senses.zh_tr: 那場馬戲團的票賣得太貴，且銷售得很差。

senses.en: We have dealt with the fisherman for a long time.
senses.zh_tr: 我們已經和那漁夫做了很久的生意了。

senses.en: It's very difficult for me to deal with all the problems by myself.
senses.zh_tr: 要獨自處理所有的問題對我而言很困難。

senses.en: We made a deal with that company to supply cotton for export.
senses.zh_tr: 我們和那家公司達成協議，由他們供應棉花為出口之用。

senses.en: The printer cost me two thousand and three hundred dollars.
senses.zh_tr: 這臺印表機花了我兩千三百元。

senses.en: Traveling by plane is more expensive than by train.
senses.zh_tr: 搭飛機旅行比搭火車貴多了。

senses.en: Can you lend me some money? = Can you lend some money to me?
senses.zh_tr: 可以借我一些錢嗎？

senses.en: The bank loaned me some money to buy the house.
senses.zh_tr: 銀行貸款給我去買房子。

senses.en: When can you pay me back?
senses.zh_tr: 你何時可以把錢還給我？

senses.en: The price of that purse is too high.
senses.zh_tr: 那個女用包的價錢很高。

notes: 延伸：價錢只有高低（The price is high/low.）。絕對不可以用 expensive 或 cheap 來形容價錢。

senses.en: Everybody wished to win first prize of the lottery.
senses.zh_tr: 每個人希望能贏得彩券的第一頭獎。

phrases.zh: 中頭彩（jackpot 指的是可贏得的最大賭注）
phrases.example: What would you do if you hit the jackpot?

senses.en: We should teach the kids how to save.
senses.zh_tr: 我們應該教小孩如何儲蓄。

senses.en: It will save money if you walk to the office instead of taking the bus.
senses.zh_tr: 如果你走路去上班的話，就可以省下搭公車的錢。

senses.en: The hero finally saved all the villagers in the film.
senses.zh_tr: 在影片裡，英雄最後拯救了所有的村民。

derived.example: I used my savings to buy a new car.

senses.en: I am willing to sell my house if anyone wants to buy it.
senses.zh_tr: 我很願意把我的房子賣掉，假如有人要買的話。

senses.en: They spent a lot of time and money building the skyscraper.
senses.zh_tr: 他們花了很多時間和金錢建造這棟摩天大樓。

notes: 延伸辨析：spend、cost、take 都有「花費」的意思，但用法不太一樣。spend 一定要人當主詞，且除了花錢以外，也可以花時間。至於事物（或虛主詞 it）當主詞的花時間或花錢則要用 take。而 cost 一定要事物（或虛主詞 it）當主詞，且指的只有花錢。例：I spent $100 buying the book. ＝ The book cost me $100 to buy it. ＝ It cost me $100 to buy the book.／I spent one hour reading the novel. ＝ The novel took me one hour to read. ＝ It took me one hour to read the novel.

senses.en: The bill for electricity is always higher than the bill for water.
senses.zh_tr: 電費的帳單總是比水費的帳單高多了。

senses.en: I only have some big bills. Can you pay for me first?
senses.zh_tr: 我只有一些大鈔。你可否先幫我付？

senses.en: The new education bill will appear next month.
senses.zh_tr: 新的教育法案將會在下個月施行。

senses.en: Do you want to pay by cash or credit card?
senses.zh_tr: 你要付現金還是刷卡？

senses.en: I need to cash a check at the bank later.
senses.zh_tr: 我待會要去銀行兌現一張支票。

derived.example: Her job is a cashier who takes and gives out money at that restaurant.

senses.en: When you buy the jeans, there should be no charge for alterations.
senses.zh_tr: 當你買牛仔褲時，修改長度應該是不會收取費用的。

senses.en: How much do you charge for a room for the night?
senses.zh_tr: 這個晚上一間房要收費多少錢？

phrases.phrase: to be in charge of ＝ to take care of
phrases.example: I was in charge of my younger brother when my parents were away.

senses.en: The couple threw a coin into the pond to make a wish.
senses.zh_tr: 這對情侶把硬幣丟到池塘裡許願。

senses.en: How many credit cards do you have?
senses.zh_tr: 你有幾張信用卡？

derived.example: The old man's credit is very good.

senses.en: The American teachers in China are paid in local currency.
senses.zh_tr: 在中國，美國教師的工資是用中國貨幣所支付。

senses.en: The company was in debt to me. = The company owed me money.
senses.zh_tr: 這家公司欠我錢。

phrases.example: She was in debt to me when she still studied, but she has been out of debt since she made some money.

senses.en: In order to earn more money, he found another job in the evening.
senses.zh_tr: 為了要賺更多錢，他在晚上找了另一份工作。

senses.en: The insurance company paid all her medical fees when she was ill in the hospital.
senses.zh_tr: 這家保險公司付了她生病住院時所有的醫療費用。

senses.en: He had to pay a NT$3,000 fine because he ran through the traffic light.
senses.zh_tr: 他必須要繳付新臺幣三千元的罰金，因他闖紅燈。

senses.en: He was fined NT$3,000.
senses.zh_tr: 他被罰款三千元。

senses.en: The banker was not satisfied with her income and decided to quit.
senses.zh_tr: 這位銀行家不滿意她的薪水，並且決定要辭職。

senses.en: His company had big losses last year.
senses.zh_tr: 他的公司去年出現巨額虧損。

senses.en: Have you reported the loss of your car to the police?
senses.zh_tr: 你已向警方報失車子了嗎？

exercises.answer_source: 答案已依課本解答頁核對（2026-07-10）

reading.title: Reading Comprehension

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.text: Did you feel anyone steal your wallet?

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.text: How much was in your wallet?

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.text: I had NT$5,000 before I bought the purse for my girlfriend.

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.text: How much did it cost you?

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.text: That's too bad! So you really lost a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.text: You're right. Besides, all of my cards were lost, including the identification card, the national health insurance card, and three credit cards.

dialogue.speaker: Tina
dialogue.text: Don't worry. Maybe the police will give you good news in a few days.

reading.note: national health insurance card 全民健保卡

questions.q: What did Terry buy for his girlfriend?
How much money did Terry lose?
How many cards did Terry lose?
How did Terry lose his wallet?
options: He gave it to his girlfriend.
He threw it to the trash can.

questions.q: It ________ me one month to visit the USA.
It ________ Tim NT$30,000 to go to England last month.
I ________ two hundred dollars buying the coat last night.
They ________ me NT$2,000 for a double room for a night.
I ________ five books from the library for my summer vacation last night.
Would you please ________ me your scooter?
He is heavily in ________ because he likes gambling.（gambling 賭博）
The waitress wasn't satisfied with her ________ and quit her job last month.
He forgot to pay the phone ________, so he can't make a call now.
The writer ________ NT$1,000,000 a month by writing some novels.
I don't mind ________ to have my cellphone fixed.
The driver was ________ NT$12,000 because he was driving drunk.
What is the ________ of this diamond ring? It must be very high.
The man only receives low ________ because he doesn't have a good educational background.
It'll ________ some time if we take a taxi instead of walking.
The student is so independent that he is able to ________ with the problems by himself.
Vicky: How much do you have in ________?  Tim: I only have big bills.
